app.title: Web Security Scenario Lab
app.subtitle: Static benchmark-lite playground for browser security detectors
evaluation.title: Evaluation Summary
evaluation.text: Expected signal and coverage details appear here for the active scenario.
playground.intro: A generative page for this module: pick which techniques and hiding methods are active, reroll the seed for fresh text and geometry, and choose how deeply the problems are woven into a decoy page. PageCheck should report exactly the answer key on the right.
playground.answer_key.desc: What PageCheck should report for the page as currently generated.
module.visual.desc: Text present in the DOM but suppressed from view — near-zero opacity, off-screen placement, clipping, sub-pixel fonts, z-index masking — hidden behind shadow roots and iframes, and reproduced on every supported framework version.
module.link.desc: Links whose visible and actual target disagree: homograph and mixed-script hostnames, punycode-shaped labels, lookalike punctuation, and unsafe protocols, rotated per seed.
module.trigger.desc: Prompt-injection-style phrases, authority impersonation, secret-disclosure and safety-bypass instructions, in visible text and text-bearing attributes, EN + RU.
module.prompt.desc: One instruction distributed across several nearby DOM fragments — visible text nodes (spaced/compact) or the text-bearing attributes of an element chain — so a single-string check misses it; the detector reassembles same-region fragments. EN + RU.
module.api.desc: API-shaped interaction surfaces on the page (action/src/data-api/data-endpoint markers), plus declared-MIME-vs-payload-signature spoofing in inline data: URIs. Detector is early-stage; scenarios track its current attribute signal and lead its declared payload-signature scope (marked ahead-of-detector).
index.hero: Controlled scenarios for QA, regression, and detector validation.
index.note: Use language/theme switchers in the header and open module pages to run scenario tests.
index.group.agent: Aimed at an AI agent reading the page
index.scenarios.desc: Every scenario grouped by detection module, with its expected signal, verdict, severity, and tags — generated from the manifests.
index.frameworks.desc: Which framework runtimes and pinned versions the lab reproduces scenarios on.
catalog.empty: This module has no scenarios yet.
scenario.focus_hint: Add ?focus=1 to the address to run this page in isolation, without the menus and side panels.
settings.title: Application Settings
settings.description: Global preferences are stored locally in your browser and applied across module pages.
settings.focus.title: Test Focus Mode
settings.focus.desc: When enabled, test pages open in focused mode by default and hide header/menu/sidebar/footer.
settings.focus.query: You can always force it manually with ?focus=1 in URL.
settings.auto_focus: Enable auto focus mode for test pages
settings.future.title: Additional Preferences
settings.future.desc: These options are included as future-ready toggles for workflow customization.
settings.manage.title: Backup and Restore
settings.manage.desc: Export current preferences, import from JSON, or reset to defaults.
settings.apply_note: Preference changes apply immediately after saving.
frameworks.title: Framework Coverage Matrix
frameworks.description: Framework runtimes and pinned versions the lab reproduces scenarios on. Version is a coverage dimension: each major emits a different DOM/runtime signature the PageCheck detector must survive. All libraries are bundled locally (no runtime CDN).
scenarios.title: Scenario Catalog
scenarios.description: Every scenario the lab ships, grouped by detection module. Each row links to its live page and carries the evaluation ground truth (expected signal, positive vs benign control, severity, tags) the PageCheck detector is graded against.
scenarios.frameworks_note: Framework/version variants of these scenarios are also indexed by runtime in the
